title: vLLM on ROCm Custom Plugin Boundary

problem.summary: Sketch the boundary where a vLLM on ROCm plugin or kernel modification specializes decode, sampling, normalization, or KV-cache work for a fixed serving contract.
inputs: logits, hidden states, or KV-cache tensors
plugin fields describing dtype, head layout, beam width, and block size
outputs: plugin output tensor or updated KV-cache state
serving-stage latency record with plugin metadata
constraints: Template-only; no vLLM on ROCm, FlashInfer, vLLM, or custom plugin win is claimed
Plugin enqueue must use the provided framework GPU stream and avoid allocation or synchronization
Record model, vLLM on ROCm version, batching policy, KV-cache policy, precision, and timing boundary

verification.method: Compare plugin outputs with vLLM on ROCm or framework references for decode, sampling, normalization, and KV-cache edge cases before timing.

recommended_shapes: batch=32 heads=32 kv_heads=8 head_dim=128 block=16 paged KV

curation.notes: ROCm parity port from CUDA task `tensorrt-llm-custom-plugin`. This is a template/harness scaffold only; CUDA-origin result artifacts were intentionally not copied, and no ROCm timing, correctness, or profiler-counter evidence is claimed yet. Run on AMD hardware, attach ROCm version, hipcc flags, gfx target, and label HIP-event-only results as timing-only.
